Phase 5 completed: session-wide multiple-testing reconciliation consumed the 20 executed trials already in the ledger and confirmed that zero family-level survivors propagated into a session-level candidate set. Phase 6 completed: the golden-egg report closed the hunt with zero GOLD and zero SILVER candidates. The highest-EV output of the session is the posture-shortening PCC evidence, not an orthogonal deployable hedge; the fork memo therefore recommends escalating PCC findings rather than expanding candidate promotion.

Phase 7 completed: PCC evidence was condensed into a one-page escalation memo for the baseline workstream. It explicitly rules out a generic Phase B calibration bug, confirms grandfathering is provisional-only, and prioritizes TOKYO_OPEN, SINGAPORE_OPEN, then US_DATA_1000 for clean Mode A rediscovery once Terminal 1 resolves posture.
